message.content:
Investigate a reported bug in the DesignFlow Angular frontend at C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend. Read-only; report findings with exact file:line citations. Do NOT edit anything.

BUG: The user created a "sample" (sample tracking) via the create dialog, the creation appeared to succeed, but nothing showed up afterward — the new sample did not appear in the list/grid.

Relevant files:
- src/app/pages/sample_tracking/create-dialog/create-dialog.component.ts and .html (the create flow / submit handler / what it returns on close)
- src/app/pages/sample_tracking/ (the parent component that opens the dialog and is supposed to refresh the grid after creation)
- src/app/helpers/services/tracking.service.ts (the create API call)
- src/app/helpers/ag-grid/sample.tracking.config.ts (grid config / row data binding)

Determine: (1) is this a real/confirmable defect? (2) root cause with file:line — e.g., after dialog closes the list isn't reloaded, the create API response isn't pushed into rowData, an applyTransaction/setRowData is missing, a filter hides it, or the create call fails silently. (3) whether it's frontend (list not refreshed) or backend (create didn't persist / returned error). Quote the key lines and trace the post-create code path.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

content.text: Good. Now let me check the tracking service to understand the createSample call:

content.content:
1	// Column config for the Sample Tracking grid skin. Mirrors the addColumn /
2	// commonDefaults factory used by the other tracking grids (e.g.
3	// license.tracking.config.ts) so the look matches the existing screens.
4	
5	import { directionLabel } from '../../pages/sample_tracking/helpers/sample.status';
6	
7	export const SAMPLE_TRACKING_COLUMN_HEADERS_CONFIG: any = {
8	  columnDefs: getSampleTrackingColumnDefs()
9	};
10	
11	function getSampleTrackingColumnDefs() {
12	  const commonDefaults = {
13	    filter: 'agTextColumnFilter',
14	    floatingFilter: true,
15	    sortable: true,
16	    editable: false,
17	    hide: false,
18	    checkboxSelection: false,
19	    rowDrag: false,
20	    rowGroup: false,
21	    cellStyle: { 'text-align': 'center' }
22	  };
23	
24	  const addColumn = (
25	    headerName: string,
26	    field: string,
27	    colId: string,
28	    width: number,
29	    overrides: any = {}
30	  ) => ({
31	    headerName,
32	    field,
33	    colId,
34	    col_id: colId, // keep both for backwards-compat with the layout system
35	    width,
36	    ...commonDefaults,
37	    ...overrides
38	  });
39	
40	  return [
41	    addColumn('Photo', 'primary_photo', 'primary_photo', 90, {
42	      cellRenderer: 'samplePhotoRenderer',
43	      editable: false,
44	      filter: false,
45	      floatingFilter: false,
46	      sortable: false
47	    }),
48	    // expand/collapse for the master-detail (event timeline + comments)
49	    addColumn('Sample', 'sample_name', 'sample_name', 220, {
50	      cellRenderer: 'agGroupCellRenderer',
51	      editable: true
52	    }),
53	    addColumn('Status', 'status', 'status', 180, {
54	      cellRenderer: 'sampleStatusRenderer',
55	      editable: false
56	    }),
57	    addColumn('Office', 'office_location', 'office_location', 110),
58	    addColumn('Next Stop', 'next_stop', 'next_stop', 120),
59	    addColumn('Final Destination', 'final_destination', 'final_destination', 150),
60	    addColumn('Customer', 'customer_name', 'customer_name', 180, {
61	      editable: false
62	    }),
63	    addColumn('Courier', 'courier', 'courier', 110, {
64	      editable: true,
65	      cellEditor: 'agSelectCellEditor',
66	      cellEditorParams: { values: ['ups', 'fedex', 'aae', 'bombino'] },
67	      valueFormatter: courierFormatter
68	    }),
69	    addColumn('Tracking #', 'tracking_number', 'tracking_number', 150, {
70	      cellRenderer: 'trackingLinkRenderer',
71	      editable: true
72	    }),
73	    addColumn('Box', 'box_label', 'box_label', 130),
74	    addColumn('Origin', 'origin', 'origin', 140, {
75	      filter: 'agSetColumnFilter'
76	    }),
77	    addColumn('Journey', 'direction', 'direction', 130, {
78	      valueFormatter: (p: any) => directionLabel(p.value)
79	    }),
80	    addColumn('Qty', 'quantity', 'quantity', 90, {
81	      filter: 'agNumberColumnFilter',
82	      editable: true,
83	      type: 'numericColumn'
84	    }),
85	    addColumn('Retail', 'retail_price', 'retail_price', 110, {
86	      filter: 'agNumberColumnFilter',
87	      editable: true,
88	      type: 'numericColumn',
89	      valueFormatter: moneyFormatter
90	    }),
91	    addColumn('FOB Cost', 'fob_cost', 'fob_cost', 110, {
92	      filter: 'agNumberColumnFilter',
93	      editable: true,
94	      type: 'numericColumn',
95	      valueFormatter: moneyFormatter
96	    }),
97	    addColumn('Factory', 'factory_id_fk', 'factory_id_fk', 110, {
98	      filter: 'agNumberColumnFilter'
99	    }),
100	    addColumn('Linked Item', 'item_id_fk', 'item_id_fk', 120, {
101	      filter: 'agNumberColumnFilter'
102	    }),
103	    addColumn('Prod Order', 'prod_order_no_fk', 'prod_order_no_fk', 130),
104	    addColumn('Notes', 'notes', 'notes', 240, { editable: true })
105	  ];
106	}
107	
108	function moneyFormatter(params: any): string {
109	  const v = params && params.value;
110	  if (v === null || v === undefined || v === '') return '';
111	  const n = Number(v);
112	  return isNaN(n) ? String(v) : `$${n.toFixed(2)}`;
113	}
114	
115	function courierFormatter(params: any): string {
116	  const labels: any = { ups: 'UPS', fedex: 'FedEx', aae: 'AAE', bombino: 'Bombino' };
117	  return labels[String(params && params.value || '').toLowerCase()] || (params && params.value) || '';
118	}
119

content.text: Let me look at the backend API reference for the sample create endpoint:

content.content:
1	# Designflow Core Backend API Reference
2	
3	This service is the core Designflow PLM API. It handles RFQ workflows, vendors,
4	customers, grid layouts, admin settings, auth, files/images, comments,
5	notifications, tariff lookups, CBP CROSS lookup, and AI proxy calls.
6	
7	In deployed environments callers should normally use the BFF:
8	
9	```text
10	https://api.designflow.app/api/core/<core-backend-path>
11	```
12	
13	Direct local base:
14	
15	```text
16	http://localhost:5000
17	```
18	
19	## Auth
20	
21	Most routes require a Designflow JWT. The backend accepts the token in
22	`Authorization`. When called through the BFF, the BFF sends the user JWT as
23	`X-User-Authorization`, and the backend remaps it to `Authorization`.
24	
25	Common roles:
26	
27	```text
28	admin, sourcing_manager, sales, designer, vendor, production
29	```
30	
31	Routes without `authRole(...)` are either login/token routes, webhooks, or legacy
32	utility routes. Treat unauthenticated legacy routes as internal unless explicitly
33	approved for external use.
34	
35	## Route Mounts
36	
37	| Mount path | Router | Purpose |
38	|---|---|---|
39	| `/` | `lib.router.js` | Legacy RFQ/product/vendor/grid/file endpoints |
40	| `/std` | `std.router.js` | Standardized product workflow |
41	| `/api/grid` | `grid.router.js` | Grid access metadata |
42	| `/customers` | `customer-core.router.js` | Core-facing customer endpoints |
43	| `/api/customers` | `customer.router.js` | Customer CRUD |
44	| `/api/item-size` | `itemSize.router.js` | Item size CRUD |
45	| `/api/item_depth` | `itemDepth.router.js` | Item depth CRUD |
46	| `/` | `digitalocean.router.js` | File/image storage helpers |
47	| `/api/cross` | `cross.router.js` | CBP CROSS search proxy |
48	| `/api/openrouter` | `openrouter.router.js` | AI provider/model proxy |
49	| `/api/comment` | `comment.router.js` | Comments |
50	| `/api/role` | `role.router.js` | Role and UI permission metadata |
51	| `/api/admin` | `admin.router.js` | Admin reference data, tariffs, settings, impersonation |
52	| `/api/autofill` | `autofill.router.js` | Item autofill data |
53	| `/api/user` | `user.router.js` | User profile/photo |
54	| `/auth` | `auth.router.js` | Azure SSO |
55	| `/api/notifications` | `notification.router.js` | Notifications |
56	| `/api` | `health.router.js` | Health check |
57	
58	## Health and Auth
59	
60	| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
61	|---|---|---|---|
62	| `GET` | `/api/health` | none | Service health |
63	| `POST` | `/auth/azure` | none | Azure SSO callback/login |
64	| `GET` | `/verifyToken` | user JWT | Verify current token |
65	| `POST` | `/verifySingleUseToken` | none | Verify single-use vendor/signup token |
66	| `POST` | `/signup` | none | Signup flow |
67	| `POST` | `/forgotPassw` | none | Forgot password flow |
68	| `POST` | `/changePassword` | none | Password change flow |
69	
70	## Core RFQ, Vendor, Product, and Grid Routes
71	
72	These legacy routes are mounted at the service root.
73	
74	| Method | Path | Purpose |
75	|---|---|---|
76	| `POST` | `/viewlayout` | Get grid view layout |
77	| `POST` | `/getRFQViewlayout` | Get RFQ view layout |
78	| `POST` | `/getAllItem` | Get RFQ/items grid rows |
79	| `GET` | `/getAllItems` | Get all item records |
80	| `POST` | `/getItemImages` | Get item images |
81	| `POST` | `/findRFQItemByPriceCode` | Find RFQ item by price code |
82	| `POST` | `/getRFQItemById` | Get RFQ item by id |
83	| `POST` | `/getRfqItemHistory` | Get RFQ copy/history lineage |
84	| `POST` | `/checkRfqCopyAllowed` | Validate RFQ copy rules |
85	| `POST` | `/addRfqItem` | Create RFQ item |
86	| `POST` | `/updateRfqItem` | Update RFQ item |
87	| `POST` | `/archiveRFQItem` | Archive / un-archive RFQ item (per-vendor `RFQVendor_archived` for vendors; global `rfqItem_archive` for internal roles) |
88	| `POST` | `/deleteRFQItem` | Delete RFQ item |
89	| `GET` | `/getRFQGroup` | Get RFQ groups |
90	| `POST` | `/addNewRFQGroup` | Create RFQ group |
91	| `GET` | `/getRFQStep` | Get RFQ workflow steps |
92	| `POST` | `/setRFQSteps` | Update RFQ workflow steps |
93	| `POST` | `/getMerchGroup` | Get merch group data |
94	| `POST` | `/getMG` | Get merch group data |
95	| `GET` | `/getCompanyCode` | Get company codes |
96	| `GET` | `/findLicenseList` | Get license list |
97	| `POST` | `/addLicenseList` | Add license list entry |
98	| `PATCH` | `/updateLicenseList/:id` | Update license list entry |
99	| `GET` | `/findDeliveryLocation` | Get delivery locations |
100	| `POST` | `/addDeliveryLocation` | Add delivery location |
